title: orobanche minor var. hyalina
intro: Orobanche minor var. hyalina, also known as the lesser broomrape, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family, obtaining its nutrients by connecting to the roots of its host plants. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Orobanche minor var. hyalina include its ability to relieve congestion, reduce fever, and soothe sore throats. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be extracted and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
